data.content:
The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`), but it's never added to the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler doesn't know about it.

The fix is to add the generated file to `@(Compile)` inside the target:

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to the compilation so the compiler picks it up -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file before `CoreCompile`, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's `CoreCompile` needs the file to be in the `@(Compile)` items to include it in compilation.

The target needs to add an `<ItemGroup>` with a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` after writing the file, so the compiler knows about it.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to the compilation so the compiler picks it up -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

    <!-- Add the generated file to the compilation so the compiler picks it up -->
    <ItemGroup>

    <ItemGroup>

    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. The issue was that the `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never added it to MSBuild's `@(Compile)` item group. Without that, the C# compiler didn't know the file existed.

The fix: added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
